does: This paper introduces EvalVerse, a comprehensive evaluation framework designed to assess professional cinematic video generation beyond basic prompt-following accuracy. Rather than treating evaluation as a simple engineering task, the authors develop a pipeline-aware system that incorporates expert-calibrated metrics covering cinematic quality dimensions including acting, aesthetics, and production values. The framework systematizes subjective cinematic expertise into measurable evaluation criteria, addressing the critical gap between automated metrics and human aesthetic perception. EvalVerse organizes domain knowledge into a structured taxonomy aligned with professional filmmaking standards, enabling reliable assessment of generative video models that increasingly leverage reinforcement learning and agentic workflows for high-quality synthesis.
connects: This work builds on the broader challenge of evaluating generative models, extending beyond text-to-image and language model benchmarks toward the more complex domain of video synthesis. It responds to limitations in existing video generation benchmarks that focus narrowly on instruction-following rather than perceptual quality, similar to how vision-language evaluation evolved from basic accuracy metrics toward aesthetic and semantic understanding. The paper connects to prior research on expert-calibrated evaluation systems and human-AI alignment in assessment, while acknowledging the shift in video generation toward RL-based training and agentic workflows that require more sophisticated evaluation signals than supervised learning approaches.
enables: EvalVerse facilitates the development and optimization of professional-grade video generation systems by providing trustworthy evaluation signals that can guide reinforcement learning training and agentic decision-making. The framework enables researchers to systematically improve cinematic quality dimensions beyond prompt adherence, supporting the transition from foundational models to production-ready synthesis. By digitizing cinematic expertise into quantifiable metrics, the work opens pathways for automated quality feedback loops, human-in-the-loop refinement systems, and comparative benchmarking across competing video generation approaches. This foundation could also inform downstream applications in film production, advertising, and creative industries where aesthetic quality and technical mastery are paramount.
